{{Infobox concept
| name = Territory
| introduced = House of Blades
}}

''See also: [[Territory Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Territories]] are distinct worlds or realms that exist apart from the [[Unnamed World]]. They can be accessed through openings known as [[Gates]], which serve as connections between the primary world and these specific domains. While nine original [[Territories]] have existed since the beginning of time, representing various natural powers, a tenth was later established called [[Elysia]] to represent human virtues. Some [[Territories]], such as [[Valinhall]], are specialized for combat against other [[Travelers]]. A Territory can be a vast expanse or contained entirely within the walls of a single building. A Territory is typically embodied by a single [[Incarnation]], a person who has lost control and become a manifestation of that realm's power and nature. These [[Incarnations]] can warp the reality around them to reflect their Territory's [[essence]]. However, staying within a Territory for too long can cause an [[Incarnation]] to be absorbed into its fabric. While most [[Territories]] require [[Travelers]] to intentionally enter them through [[Gates]] to gain specific advantages, [[Elysia]] is noted as an exception where such access can occur unexpectedly. [[Territories]] are often used as tools for commerce, trade, and warfare, though they were once considered wild and free.}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|A Territory is a power summoned by a [[Traveler]] from an unknown location that can be accessed through a Gate. These realms, which may manifest as anything from a moon-lit wasteland to a house containing an entire world, operate under their own unique rules and restrictions where time flows differently. Within these spaces, normal physical laws barely hold sway, creating an environment where magical events are expected to occur. A [[Traveler]] can call upon this power using a pattern or by answering a mental call, as seen when [[Alin]] uses borrowed strength to reinforce a shield. [[Simon]] eventually gains the power of a Territory through [[liquid steel]] and cool breath, commanding a realm that contains a [[Damascan]] family.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|A [[Territory]] can answer a mental call.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2">''House of Blades'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|A [[Territory]] is a power that a [[Traveler]] summons from somewhere.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4">''House of Blades'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|The characters are currently in a [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|A [[Territory]] is a place accessible through a Gate.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c6">''House of Blades'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|A [[Territory]] can be a house that contains an entire world within its walls.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Time flows differently in each [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Each [[Territory]] has its own rules and restrictions.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c7">''House of Blades'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|A [[Territory]] is a place where magical things are supposed to happen.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c8">''House of Blades'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|In a [[Territory]], normal physical laws barely hold sway.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c12">''House of Blades'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Cool breath]] and [[liquid steel]] grant [[Simon]] the power of a [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] has a [[Territory]] containing a [[Damascan]] family.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16">''House of Blades'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|A [[Traveler]] can call upon their [[Territory]] using a pattern.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|A moon-lit wasteland may be someone's [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c17">''House of Blades'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] uses the borrowed power of his [[Territory]] to reinforce a shield.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c20">''House of Blades'', chapter 20</ref>}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|A Territory is a specific type of domain or location that encompasses all the powers of nature. Within these realms, individuals like [[Simon]] can draw upon increased speed and strength, though certain items such as the [[frozen horn]] lose their functionality. While [[Naraka]] [[Travelers]] may eventually learn to maintain composure within these spaces, the presence of a Territory serves to keep an [[Incarnation]] in check. Furthermore, [[Kai]] possesses the ability to reach through his [[sword]] to access his own domain, and objects like the glass bell can be used to banish entities back to their respective [[Territories]].}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Kai]] can reach through his [[sword]] to his [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Territories]] encompass all the powers of nature.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] was in his [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] can draw strength and speed from his [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[frozen horn]] does not work in a [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The glass bell banishes things back to their [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c18">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Naraka]] [[Travelers]] may learn to stay cool in their [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c19">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|A [[Territory]] is a type of location or domain.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The presence of the [[Territory]] helps keep the [[Incarnation]] in check.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 23</ref>}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A Territory functions as a realm that grants its possessor supernatural vision. While [[Alin]] possesses the ability to transmit energy directly to his Territory, [[Ilana]] only realizes the nature of such a space after falling into one. Within these realms, [[Incarnations]] may pursue their own objectives by exploiting foreign [[Travelers]]. The entity known as [[Indirial]] was once sealed inside a Territory, and [[Simon]] remains bound to his own by a chain, leaving him in significant debt to the realm.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A [[Territory]] provides supernatural vision.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c6">''City of Light'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] can send energy directly to his [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7">''City of Light'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Ilana]] recognizes a [[Territory]] when she falls into one.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c15">''City of Light'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Incarnations]] existing within [[Territories]] can advance their own goals at the expense of foreign [[Travelers]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c18">''City of Light'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] was sealed into the [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c24">''City of Light'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]] is in debt to his [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A chain binds [[Simon]] to his [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|“Then that Damascan family. You have them. In your Territory.”<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“I was in my Territory,” Simon said carefully.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Concepts]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate concepts]]
[[Category:Terminology]]
